beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check messages at home.
beats.reasoning: Zoe starts Monday morning at full energy. Early wake to enjoy the sunny day ahead and mentally prep for work week.

beats.activity: Drive from Bellevue to SoDo bookstore.
beats.reasoning: 40-min drive across town to open the bookstore; Bel-Red to SoDo is a standard commute despite Light Rail closure (doesn't affect driving).

beats.activity: Open bookstore, stock shelves, handle customer orders.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift at the bookstore before the FIFA match; Zoe's a sarcastic thrill-seeker, so the 11am match is on her radar but not her priority yet.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA match at a downtown bar with close friends.
beats.reasoning: Zoe values time with close friends over crowds; catching the 11am match live at a bar fits her thrill-seeking vibe and sunny Monday energy. She's intentional about social time.
Return to work after the match; 40-min drive back across town.

beats.activity: Grab lunch: sandwich and espresso at a nearby cafe.
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch break in SoDo before afternoon shift; she's back at work by early afternoon.

beats.activity: Afternoon and evening shift: manage bookstore, chat with regulars, handle inventory.
beats.reasoning: Solid 4.5-hour block to close out the workday; Zoe runs a quiet neighborhood bookstore with loyal customers, so she likely enjoys the steady rhythm.

beats.activity: Drive home from SoDo to Bellevue.
beats.reasoning: 45-min drive home after work; evening light is still strong in June at 7:30pm, and Zoe's energy is steady.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Light dinner at home; Zoe is a planner and values quiet time after a full day.

diary:
Woke up early with actual energy—the kind of Monday that doesn't usually happen. Coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because the sun was already doing its thing through the kitchen window. Drive to SoDo was smooth; I think everyone was too distracted by the World Cup buzz to clog the roads.

The bookstore felt alive this morning. Got the shelves restocked and actually had a moment to chat with Mrs. Chen about her book club picks before I had to dash out. Couldn't miss the USA-Wales match at Belltown Pub with the crew. The place was packed, everyone screaming at the screen—I got caught up in it more than I expected. Good energy, at least.

Back to work after, which felt a bit anticlimactic, but that's how it goes. Grabbed a sandwich at Voula's and just sat there for a bit, decompressing. The afternoon crowd kept me busy enough. Drove home as the light was getting golden, feeling tired but satisfied. Made pasta, nothing fancy. Bed early.
thought_process: I almost skipped the World Cup thing—opening-day logistics and all—but I realized I've been tethered to the bookstore too much lately, and my friends were counting on me. The good weather and early bar opening made it feel like an actual event worth stepping away for. I think I needed that break more than I admitted. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but I noticed fewer people downtown than usual, which made the pub feel more intimate somehow. By the afternoon shift, I was recharged but not restless, just settled into the rhythm of inventory and regulars asking for recommendations. The day felt balanced—work and play, neither taking over the other.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
